tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with a scholarship deadline tomorrow and a fun activity available now. I must decide whether to spend focused hours on the essay or enjoy the immediate solo option.

Starting the scholarship application now creates a buffer for potential errors, significantly reducing Thursday's stress. Since the fun activity can be postponed without loss, I should prioritize the application to optimize my limited time.
Despite working 2 hours, progress is only 42% with half my free time lost. The essay remains incomplete, leaving me significantly behind schedule and increasing stress.
I missed the window for the fun activity, and my scholarship progress remains behind schedule with high stress.
Working one hour advanced progress slightly, but I am still behind schedule with high stress and sleep debt.
After 1.5 hours, progress improved slightly to 66%, but I remain behind schedule with high stress and only 2 hours of sleep debt.
After 1.5 hours, my scholarship progress increased slightly to 72%, yet I remain behind schedule with high stress and significant sleep debt.
Progressed to 78% in 1.5 hours, but still behind the 3-hour deadline with high stress and 2 hours sleep debt.
After 1 hour, progress rose from 78% to 82%, but I am still behind the 3-hour deadline with high stress and significant sleep debt.
Progress jumped from 82% to 90% in two hours, nearing the 3-hour deadline. However, stress and sleep debt remain high despite this improvement.
Progress increased to 94% in one hour, nearing the deadline. However, high stress and two hours of sleep debt remain significant constraints.
Progress reached 97% in one hour, but high stress and two hours of sleep debt remain significant constraints before the deadline.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
